title: ranunculus propinquus var. subborealis
intro: Ranunculus propinquus var. subborealis, also known as Great Lobelia or Kamahi, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Japan and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. The health benefits of Ranunculus propinquus var. subborealis include its ability to aid in the relief of pain and inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in traditional herbal remedies to treat a variety of ailments.
